# OWL Restrictions

ASG maps OWL class restrictions to OpenAPI schema constraints. This document covers all 17 restriction types recognized by ASG.

## Restriction Types Overview

| Category | OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Effect |
|----------|----------------|----------------|
| **Cardinality** | MinCardinality | `minItems`, required if ≥1 |
| | MaxCardinality | `maxItems` |
| | ExactCardinality | `minItems` + `maxItems` |
| **Value** | SomeValuesFrom | `minItems: 1`, required |
| | AllValuesFrom | Type constraint only |
| | HasValue | `default` value |
| | OneOf | `enum` (individuals) |
| **Data Cardinality** | DataMinCardinality | `minItems`, required if ≥1 |
| | DataMaxCardinality | `maxItems` |
| | DataExactCardinality | `minItems` + `maxItems` |
| **Data Value** | DataSomeValuesFrom | `minItems: 1`, required |
| | DataAllValuesFrom | Type constraint only |
| | DataHasValue | `default` value |
| | DataOneOf | `enum` (literals) |
| **Boolean** | UnionOf | `anyOf` |
| | IntersectionOf | `allOf` |
| | ComplementOf | `not` |

## Cardinality Restrictions

### MinCardinality (Object Properties)

Sets minimum number of values required.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasAddress"/>
    <owl:minCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:minCardinality>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
hasAddress:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Address'
```

**Requiredness:** If `minCardinality ≥ 1` and `enforceRequiredness: true`, the property is added to `required`.

### MaxCardinality (Object Properties)

Sets maximum number of values allowed.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/primaryPhone"/>
    <owl:maxCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:maxCardinality>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
primaryPhone:
  type: array
  maxItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
```

**Note:** `maxCardinality: 1` still generates an array with `maxItems: 1`. For true singular properties, use `exactCardinality: 1` which ASG may optimize to a non-array.

### ExactCardinality (Object Properties)

Sets exact number of values required.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/supervisor"/>
    <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
    <owl:onClass rdf:resource="https://example.com/Manager"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
supervisor:
  minItems: 1
  maxItems: 1
  # For cardinality 1, may be optimized to non-array
```

### DataMinCardinality / DataMaxCardinality / DataExactCardinality

Same as above but for data properties (literals).

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/phoneNumber"/>
    <owl:minCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:minCardinality>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
phoneNumber:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
```

## Value Restrictions

### SomeValuesFrom (Object Properties)

Requires at least one value from the specified class.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasSkill"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Skill"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
hasSkill:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Skill'
```

**Requiredness:** Always implies required when `enforceRequiredness: true`.

### AllValuesFrom (Object Properties)

Constrains all values to be from the specified class (type constraint only).

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasEmail"/>
    <owl:allValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmailAddress"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
hasEmail:
  type: array
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/EmailAddress'
# No minItems - property is optional
```

**Note:** `allValuesFrom` does NOT imply cardinality. It only constrains the type when values exist.

### HasValue (Object Properties)

Requires a specific individual value.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/country"/>
    <owl:hasValue rdf:resource="https://example.com/USA"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
country:
  type: string
  default: "USA"
```

### OneOf (Object Properties - Enumeration)

Restricts to one of several named individuals.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Active"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Inactive"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Pending"/>
            </owl:oneOf>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
status:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Active
    - Inactive
    - Pending
```

### DataSomeValuesFrom / DataAllValuesFrom / DataHasValue / DataOneOf

Same semantics as object property versions, but for literal values.

**DataOneOf Example (OWL):**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/priority"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <rdfs:Datatype>
            <owl:oneOf>
                <rdf:List>
                    <rdf:first rdf:datatype="xsd:string">low</rdf:first>
                    <rdf:rest>
                        <rdf:List>
                            <rdf:first rdf:datatype="xsd:string">medium</rdf:first>
                            <rdf:rest>
                                <rdf:List>
                                    <rdf:first rdf:datatype="xsd:string">high</rdf:first>
                                    <rdf:rest rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#nil"/>
                                </rdf:List>
                            </rdf:rest>
                        </rdf:List>
                    </rdf:rest>
                </rdf:List>
            </owl:oneOf>
        </rdfs:Datatype>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
priority:
  type: string
  enum:
    - low
    - medium
    - high
```

## Boolean Combinations

### UnionOf

Allows values from any of several classes.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Email"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Phone"/>
            </owl:unionOf>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
contact:
  anyOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Email'
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Phone'
```

### IntersectionOf

Requires values satisfying all specified classes.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/seniorManager"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:intersectionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Manager"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/SeniorStaff"/>
            </owl:intersectionOf>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
seniorManager:
  allOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Manager'
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/SeniorStaff'
```

### ComplementOf

Excludes values of a specified type.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/nonManager"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:complementOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/Manager"/>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
nonManager:
  not:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Manager'
```

## Requiredness Summary

With `enforceRequiredness: true`, these restrictions make a property required:

| Restriction | Required? |
|-------------|-----------|
| MinCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| ExactCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| SomeValuesFrom | Yes |
| DataMinCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| DataExactCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| DataSomeValuesFrom | Yes |
| MaxCardinality | No |
| AllValuesFrom | No |
| HasValue | No |
| OneOf | No |

## Array Generation Summary

These restrictions cause array generation (rather than singular values):

| Restriction | Generates Array? |
|-------------|------------------|
| MinCardinality(n) where n > 1 | Yes |
| MaxCardinality(n) where n > 1 | Yes |
| ExactCardinality(n) where n > 1 | Yes |
| SomeValuesFrom | Yes |
| DataSomeValuesFrom | Yes |

toolUseResult.content:
# OWL Restrictions

ASG maps OWL class restrictions to OpenAPI schema constraints. This document covers all 17 restriction types recognized by ASG.

## Restriction Types Overview

| Category | OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Effect |
|----------|----------------|----------------|
| **Cardinality** | MinCardinality | `minItems`, required if ≥1 |
| | MaxCardinality | `maxItems` |
| | ExactCardinality | `minItems` + `maxItems` |
| **Value** | SomeValuesFrom | `minItems: 1`, required |
| | AllValuesFrom | Type constraint only |
| | HasValue | `default` value |
| | OneOf | `enum` (individuals) |
| **Data Cardinality** | DataMinCardinality | `minItems`, required if ≥1 |
| | DataMaxCardinality | `maxItems` |
| | DataExactCardinality | `minItems` + `maxItems` |
| **Data Value** | DataSomeValuesFrom | `minItems: 1`, required |
| | DataAllValuesFrom | Type constraint only |
| | DataHasValue | `default` value |
| | DataOneOf | `enum` (literals) |
| **Boolean** | UnionOf | `anyOf` |
| | IntersectionOf | `allOf` |
| | ComplementOf | `not` |

## Cardinality Restrictions

### MinCardinality (Object Properties)

Sets minimum number of values required.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasAddress"/>
    <owl:minCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:minCardinality>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
hasAddress:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Address'
```

**Requiredness:** If `minCardinality ≥ 1` and `enforceRequiredness: true`, the property is added to `required`.

### MaxCardinality (Object Properties)

Sets maximum number of values allowed.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/primaryPhone"/>
    <owl:maxCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:maxCardinality>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
primaryPhone:
  type: array
  maxItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
```

**Note:** `maxCardinality: 1` still generates an array with `maxItems: 1`. For true singular properties, use `exactCardinality: 1` which ASG may optimize to a non-array.

### ExactCardinality (Object Properties)

Sets exact number of values required.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/supervisor"/>
    <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
    <owl:onClass rdf:resource="https://example.com/Manager"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
supervisor:
  minItems: 1
  maxItems: 1
  # For cardinality 1, may be optimized to non-array
```

### DataMinCardinality / DataMaxCardinality / DataExactCardinality

Same as above but for data properties (literals).

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/phoneNumber"/>
    <owl:minCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:minCardinality>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
phoneNumber:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
```

## Value Restrictions

### SomeValuesFrom (Object Properties)

Requires at least one value from the specified class.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasSkill"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Skill"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
hasSkill:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Skill'
```

**Requiredness:** Always implies required when `enforceRequiredness: true`.

### AllValuesFrom (Object Properties)

Constrains all values to be from the specified class (type constraint only).

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasEmail"/>
    <owl:allValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmailAddress"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
hasEmail:
  type: array
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/EmailAddress'
# No minItems - property is optional
```

**Note:** `allValuesFrom` does NOT imply cardinality. It only constrains the type when values exist.

### HasValue (Object Properties)

Requires a specific individual value.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/country"/>
    <owl:hasValue rdf:resource="https://example.com/USA"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
country:
  type: string
  default: "USA"
```

### OneOf (Object Properties - Enumeration)

Restricts to one of several named individuals.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Active"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Inactive"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Pending"/>
            </owl:oneOf>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
status:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Active
    - Inactive
    - Pending
```

### DataSomeValuesFrom / DataAllValuesFrom / DataHasValue / DataOneOf

Same semantics as object property versions, but for literal values.

**DataOneOf Example (OWL):**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/priority"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <rdfs:Datatype>
            <owl:oneOf>
                <rdf:List>
                    <rdf:first rdf:datatype="xsd:string">low</rdf:first>
                    <rdf:rest>
                        <rdf:List>
                            <rdf:first rdf:datatype="xsd:string">medium</rdf:first>
                            <rdf:rest>
                                <rdf:List>
                                    <rdf:first rdf:datatype="xsd:string">high</rdf:first>
                                    <rdf:rest rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#nil"/>
                                </rdf:List>
                            </rdf:rest>
                        </rdf:List>
                    </rdf:rest>
                </rdf:List>
            </owl:oneOf>
        </rdfs:Datatype>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
priority:
  type: string
  enum:
    - low
    - medium
    - high
```

## Boolean Combinations

### UnionOf

Allows values from any of several classes.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Email"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Phone"/>
            </owl:unionOf>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
contact:
  anyOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Email'
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Phone'
```

### IntersectionOf

Requires values satisfying all specified classes.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/seniorManager"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:intersectionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Manager"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/SeniorStaff"/>
            </owl:intersectionOf>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
seniorManager:
  allOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Manager'
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/SeniorStaff'
```

### ComplementOf

Excludes values of a specified type.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/nonManager"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:complementOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/Manager"/>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
nonManager:
  not:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Manager'
```

## Requiredness Summary

With `enforceRequiredness: true`, these restrictions make a property required:

| Restriction | Required? |
|-------------|-----------|
| MinCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| ExactCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| SomeValuesFrom | Yes |
| DataMinCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| DataExactCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| DataSomeValuesFrom | Yes |
| MaxCardinality | No |
| AllValuesFrom | No |
| HasValue | No |
| OneOf | No |

## Array Generation Summary

These restrictions cause array generation (rather than singular values):

| Restriction | Generates Array? |
|-------------|------------------|
| MinCardinality(n) where n > 1 | Yes |
| MaxCardinality(n) where n > 1 | Yes |
| ExactCardinality(n) where n > 1 | Yes |
| SomeValuesFrom | Yes |
| DataSomeValuesFrom | Yes |

# XSD Datatype Mappings

ASG maps XSD (XML Schema Definition) datatypes to OpenAPI/JSON Schema types. This document lists all 46 supported datatype mappings.

## Quick Reference

| XSD Datatype | OpenAPI Type | Format | Notes |
|--------------|--------------|--------|-------|
| `xsd:string` | `string` | - | Default string type |
| `xsd:boolean` | `boolean` | - | |
| `xsd:integer` | `integer` | - | Arbitrary precision |
| `xsd:decimal` | `number` | - | Decimal numbers |
| `xsd:float` | `number` | `double` | 32-bit float |
| `xsd:double` | `number` | `double` | 64-bit float |
| `xsd:dateTime` | `string` | `date-time` | ISO 8601 |
| `xsd:date` | `string` | `date` | ISO 8601 date only |
| `xsd:time` | `string` | `time` | ISO 8601 time only |
| `xsd:anyURI` | `string` | `uri` | |

## Complete Datatype Mapping

### String Types

| XSD Datatype | OpenAPI Type | Example |
|--------------|--------------|---------|
| `xsd:string` | `string` | `"Hello World"` |
| `xsd:normalizedString` | `string` | `"normalized text"` |
| `xsd:token` | `string` | `"token"` |
| `xsd:language` | `string` | `"en-US"` |
| `xsd:Name` | `string` | `"xmlName"` |
| `xsd:NCName` | `string` | `"localName"` |
| `xsd:NMTOKEN` | `string` | `"nmtoken"` |
| `xsd:NMTOKENS` | `string` | `"token1 token2"` |
| `xsd:ID` | `string` | `"id123"` |
| `xsd:IDREF` | `string` | `"id123"` |
| `xsd:IDREFS` | `string` | `"id1 id2"` |
| `xsd:ENTITY` | `string` | `"entity"` |
| `xsd:ENTITIES` | `string` | `"ent1 ent2"` |
| `xsd:QName` | `string` | `"prefix:local"` |
| `xsd:NOTATION` | `string` | `"notation"` |
| `rdf:langString` | `string` | `"text@en"` |
| `rdfs:Literal` | `string` | Any literal |

### Numeric Types - Integer Family

| XSD Datatype | OpenAPI Type | Range | Format |
|--------------|--------------|-------|--------|
| `xsd:integer` | `integer` | Unbounded | - |
| `xsd:long` | `integer` | ±2^63 | `int64` |
| `xsd:int` | `integer` | ±2^31 | - |
| `xsd:short` | `integer` | ±2^15 | - |
| `xsd:byte` | `integer` | ±2^7 | - |
| `xsd:nonNegativeInteger` | `integer` | ≥ 0 | - |
| `xsd:positiveInteger` | `integer` | > 0 | - |
| `xsd:nonPositiveInteger` | `integer` | ≤ 0 | - |
| `xsd:negativeInteger` | `integer` | < 0 | - |
| `xsd:unsignedLong` | `integer` | 0 to 2^64-1 | - |
| `xsd:unsignedInt` | `integer` | 0 to 2^32-1 | - |
| `xsd:unsignedShort` | `integer` | 0 to 2^16-1 | - |
| `xsd:unsignedByte` | `integer` | 0 to 255 | - |

### Numeric Types - Decimal Family

| XSD Datatype | OpenAPI Type | Format | Example |
|--------------|--------------|--------|---------|
| `xsd:decimal` | `number` | - | `123.45` |
| `xsd:float` | `number` | `double` | `1.23e4` |
| `xsd:double` | `number` | `double` | `1.23e308` |

### Boolean Type

| XSD Datatype | OpenAPI Type | Values |
|--------------|--------------|--------|
| `xsd:boolean` | `boolean` | `true`, `false` |

### Date and Time Types

| XSD Datatype | OpenAPI Type | Format | Example |
|--------------|--------------|--------|---------|
| `xsd:dateTime` | `string` | `date-time` | `2025-01-15T14:30:00Z` |
| `xsd:dateTimeStamp` | `string` | `date-time` | `2025-01-15T14:30:00+00:00` |
| `xsd:date` | `string` | `date` | `2025-01-15` |
| `xsd:time` | `string` | `time` | `14:30:00` |
| `xsd:duration` | `string` | `duration` | `P3Y6M4DT12H30M5S` |
| `xsd:gYear` | `string` | - | `2025` |
| `xsd:gYearMonth` | `string` | - | `2025-01` |
| `xsd:gMonth` | `string` | - | `--01` |
| `xsd:gMonthDay` | `string` | - | `--01-15` |
| `xsd:gDay` | `string` | - | `---15` |

### Binary Types

| XSD Datatype | OpenAPI Type | Format | Notes |
|--------------|--------------|--------|-------|
| `xsd:base64Binary` | `string` | `byte` | Base64 encoded |
| `xsd:hexBinary` | `string` | - | Hex encoded |

### URI Types

| XSD Datatype | OpenAPI Type | Format | Example |
|--------------|--------------|--------|---------|
| `xsd:anyURI` | `string` | `uri` | `https://example.com` |

### Any Types

| XSD Datatype | OpenAPI Type | Notes |
|--------------|--------------|-------|
| `xsd:anyType` | `string` | Fallback to string |
| `xsd:anySimpleType` | `string` | Fallback to string |

## Example Usage

### String Property

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/name"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
name:
  type: string
  example: "name string value"
```

### Integer Property

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/age"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
age:
  type: integer
  example: 1
```

### DateTime Property

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/createdAt"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
createdAt:
  type: string
  format: date-time
  example: "2025-01-15T14:55:58+00:00"
```

### URI Property

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/website"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
website:
  type: string
  format: uri
  example: "http://www.example.com"
```

### Boolean Property

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/isActive"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#boolean"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
isActive:
  type: boolean
  example: true
```

## Auto-Generated Examples

When no `skos:example` annotation is provided, ASG generates appropriate example values:

| OpenAPI Type | Format | Generated Example |
|--------------|--------|-------------------|
| `string` | - | `"propertyName string value"` |
| `string` | `uri` | `"http://www.example.com"` |
| `string` | `date` | `"2025-01-15"` |
| `string` | `date-time` | `"2025-01-15T14:55:58+00:00"` |
| `string` | `time` | `"14:55:58+00:00"` |
| `string` | `duration` | `"P3Y6M4DT12H30M5S"` |
| `string` | `byte` | `"Ynl0ZSBhcnJvdw=="` |
| `number` | - | `1.1` |
| `integer` | - | `1` |
| `boolean` | - | `true` |

</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
age:
  type: integer
  example: 1
```

### DateTime Property

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/createdAt"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
createdAt:
  type: string
  format: date-time
  example: "2025-01-15T14:55:58+00:00"
```

### URI Property

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/website"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
website:
  type: string
  format: uri
  example: "http://www.example.com"
```

### Boolean Property

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/isActive"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#boolean"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
isActive:
  type: boolean
  example: true
```

## Auto-Generated Examples

When no `skos:example` annotation is provided, ASG generates appropriate example values:

| OpenAPI Type | Format | Generated Example |
|--------------|--------|-------------------|
| `string` | - | `"propertyName string value"` |
| `string` | `uri` | `"http://www.example.com"` |
| `string` | `date` | `"2025-01-15"` |
| `string` | `date-time` | `"2025-01-15T14:55:58+00:00"` |
| `string` | `time` | `"14:55:58+00:00"` |
| `string` | `duration` | `"P3Y6M4DT12H30M5S"` |
| `string` | `byte` | `"Ynl0ZSBhcnJvdw=="` |
| `number` | - | `1.1` |
| `integer` | - | `1` |
| `boolean` | - | `true` |

input.content:
# OWL to OpenAPI Mapping Matrix

Quick reference for how OWL constructs map to OpenAPI/JSON Schema.

## Restriction Mappings

| OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Schema Effect | Required? | Array? |
|-----------------|----------------------|-----------|--------|
| `MinCardinality(0)` | No effect | No | No |
| `MinCardinality(1)` | `minItems: 1` | Yes | No |
| `MinCardinality(n)` where n>1 | `minItems: n` | Yes | Yes |
| `MaxCardinality(1)` | `maxItems: 1` | No | No |
| `MaxCardinality(n)` where n>1 | `maxItems: n` | No | Yes |
| `ExactCardinality(1)` | `minItems: 1, maxItems: 1` | Yes | No |
| `ExactCardinality(n)` where n>1 | `minItems: n, maxItems: n` | Yes | Yes |
| `SomeValuesFrom` | `minItems: 1` | Yes | Yes |
| `AllValuesFrom` | Type constraint only | No | No |
| `HasValue` | `default: value` | No | No |
| `OneOf` (individuals) | `enum: [labels]` | No | No |
| `DataOneOf` (literals) | `enum: [values]` | No | No |
| `UnionOf` | `anyOf: [schemas]` | No | - |
| `IntersectionOf` | `allOf: [schemas]` | No | - |
| `ComplementOf` | `not: schema` | No | - |

## Property Type Mappings

| OWL Property Type | Range | OpenAPI Mapping |
|-------------------|-------|-----------------|
| Data Property | XSD type | Primitive type (see datatypes) |
| Object Property | Resource class | `string` with `x-grl-model-link` |
| Object Property | Non-resource class | `$ref` or inline schema |
| Object Property | Named individuals | `enum` with individual labels |

## Datatype Quick Reference

| XSD Type | OpenAPI Type | OpenAPI Format |
|----------|--------------|----------------|
| `string` | `string` | - |
| `boolean` | `boolean` | - |
| `integer`, `int`, `long`, etc. | `integer` | - |
| `decimal`, `float`, `double` | `number` | `double` for float/double |
| `dateTime` | `string` | `date-time` |
| `date` | `string` | `date` |
| `time` | `string` | `time` |
| `duration` | `string` | `duration` |
| `anyURI` | `string` | `uri` |
| `base64Binary` | `string` | `byte` |

## Annotation Mappings

| OWL Annotation | OpenAPI Location |
|----------------|------------------|
| `skos:prefLabel` | `title` |
| `skos:definition` | `description` |
| `skos:example` | `example` |
| `rdfs:comment` | `description` (fallback) |
| `apiLabel` | Filters which axioms are included |
| `apiTag` | `tags` on operations |
| `apiDescription` | `description` on path items |
| `apiSummary` | `summary` on path items |
| `security` | Security requirements |

## Resource to Endpoint Mapping

| Resource | Endpoint | Methods |
|----------|----------|---------|
| Class with `apiID` | `/{ResourceName}` | GET (list), POST (create) |
| Class with `apiID` | `/{ResourceName}/{id}` | GET, PUT, PATCH, DELETE |

## Schema Structure

For a resource class `Employee`:

```yaml
components:
  schemas:
    Employee:
      type: object
      title: Employee                    # from skos:prefLabel
      description: An employee record    # from skos:definition
      additionalProperties: false
      required:
        - apiID                          # always required
        - name                           # if minCardinality ≥ 1
      properties:
        apiID:
          type: string
          title: Api Id
          description: REST ID of the object
          example: apiID string value
        name:
          type: string
          title: Name
          description: Employee's full name
          example: John Doe
        department:
          type: string
          x-grl-model-link: Department   # reference to another resource
          description: Unique identifier of Department
          example: Department-12345
```

## Path Structure

For a resource `Employee`:

```yaml
paths:
  /Employee:
    summary: Employee collection
    description: Collection of Employee resources
    get:
      operationId: Employee/collection/GET
      tags: [Employee]
      parameters:
        - name: start
          in: query
        - name: limit
          in: query
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Employee Collection
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                properties:
                  items:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      $ref: '#/components/schemas/Employee'
    post:
      operationId: Employee/collection/POST
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/Employee'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: New Employee

  /Employee/{EmployeeId}:
    summary: Single Employee
    get:
      operationId: Employee/single/GET
      parameters:
        - name: EmployeeId
          in: path
          required: true
      responses:
        '200':
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Employee'
    put: ...
    patch: ...
    delete: ...
```

input.content:
# Property Mappings

This document details how OWL data properties, object properties, and taxonomy properties are mapped to OpenAPI schemas.

## Data Properties

Data properties represent literal values (strings, numbers, dates, etc.).

### Basic Mapping

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/name">
    <skos:prefLabel>Name</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>The person's full name</skos:definition>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>

<!-- On the class -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/name"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
properties:
  name:
    type: string
    title: Name
    description: The person's full name
    example: "name string value"
required:
  - name  # because of someValuesFrom
```

### Cardinality Effects

| Restriction | Schema Effect |
|-------------|---------------|
| `someValuesFrom` | `type: array`, `minItems: 1`, required |
| `allValuesFrom` | Type only, not required |
| `exactCardinality(1)` | Non-array, required |
| `minCardinality(1)` | `minItems: 1`, required |
| `maxCardinality(1)` | `maxItems: 1` |
| `minCardinality(n)` where n>1 | `type: array`, `minItems: n`, required |

### Enum from DataOneOf

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <rdfs:Datatype>
                <owl:oneOf>
                    <rdf:List>
                        <rdf:first>active</rdf:first>
                        <rdf:rest>
                            <rdf:List>
                                <rdf:first>inactive</rdf:first>
                                <rdf:rest rdf:resource="rdf:nil"/>
                            </rdf:List>
                        </rdf:rest>
                    </rdf:List>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </rdfs:Datatype>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
status:
  type: string
  enum:
    - active
    - inactive
```

### Default Values from DataHasValue

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/country"/>
        <owl:hasValue rdf:datatype="xsd:string">USA</owl:hasValue>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
country:
  type: string
  default: USA
```

## Object Properties

Object properties link to other classes (resources or non-resources).

### Reference to Resource Class

When the range is a class that is also a resource (has `apiID`), the property becomes a reference.

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employer"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Company"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
employer:
  type: string
  x-grl-model-link: Company
  description: Unique identifier of Company
  example: Company-12345
```

The `x-grl-model-link` extension indicates this is a reference to another API resource.

### Reference to Non-Resource Class

When the range is not a resource class, a `$ref` is used.

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/address"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Address"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
address:
  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Address'
```

### Array of References

With `someValuesFrom` or cardinality > 1:

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employees"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Employee"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
employees:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    x-grl-model-link: Employee
    description: Unique identifier of Employee
    example: Employee-12345
```

### Multiple Range Classes (Union)

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Person"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Organization"/>
                </owl:unionOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
contact:
  type: array
  items:
    type: object
    additionalProperties: false
    properties:
      Person:
        $ref: '#/components/schemas/Person'
      Organization:
        $ref: '#/components/schemas/Organization'
```

## Taxonomy Properties

Taxonomy properties link to named individuals, creating enum-like constraints.

### Individual-Based Enum

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Named individuals -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive">
    <skos:prefLabel>Active</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive">
    <skos:prefLabel>Inactive</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction referencing individuals -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
status:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Active
    - Inactive
```

### Class-Based Taxonomy

When the restriction references a class, individuals of that class become enum values.

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Taxonomy class -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>

<!-- Individuals of the class -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/FullTime">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Full Time</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/PartTime">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Part Time</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employmentType"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
EmploymentStatus:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Full Time
    - Part Time
```

### Array of Taxonomy Values

With `someValuesFrom` or cardinality indicating multiple values:

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
skills:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Java
      - Python
      - JavaScript
```

## Property Name Resolution

Property names are derived from the IRI:

1. Use the fragment (after `#`) if present
2. Otherwise use the last path segment
3. CamelCase is preserved

Examples:
- `https://example.com/ontology#firstName` → `firstName`
- `https://example.com/ontology/firstName` → `firstName`
- `https://example.com/has_address` → `has_address`

## Labels and Descriptions

Properties get their metadata from:

| Field | Source (priority order) |
|-------|------------------------|
| `title` | `skos:prefLabel`, `rdfs:label`, IRI short form |
| `description` | `skos:definition`, `rdfs:comment` |
| `example` | `skos:example` on axiom, `skos:example` on property, auto-generated |

<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Person"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Organization"/>
                </owl:unionOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
contact:
  type: array
  items:
    type: object
    additionalProperties: false
    properties:
      Person:
        $ref: '#/components/schemas/Person'
      Organization:
        $ref: '#/components/schemas/Organization'
```

## Taxonomy Properties

Taxonomy properties link to named individuals, creating enum-like constraints.

### Individual-Based Enum

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Named individuals -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive">
    <skos:prefLabel>Active</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive">
    <skos:prefLabel>Inactive</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction referencing individuals -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
status:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Active
    - Inactive
```

### Class-Based Taxonomy

When the restriction references a class, individuals of that class become enum values.

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Taxonomy class -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>

<!-- Individuals of the class -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/FullTime">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Full Time</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/PartTime">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Part Time</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employmentType"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
EmploymentStatus:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Full Time
    - Part Time
```

### Array of Taxonomy Values

With `someValuesFrom` or cardinality indicating multiple values:

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
skills:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Java
      - Python
      - JavaScript
```

## Property Name Resolution

Property names are derived from the IRI:

1. Use the fragment (after `#`) if present
2. Otherwise use the last path segment
3. CamelCase is preserved

Examples:
- `https://example.com/ontology#firstName` → `firstName`
- `https://example.com/ontology/firstName` → `firstName`
- `https://example.com/has_address` → `has_address`

## Labels and Descriptions

Properties get their metadata from:

| Field | Source (priority order) |
|-------|------------------------|
| `title` | `skos:prefLabel`, `rdfs:label`, IRI short form |
| `description` | `skos:definition`, `rdfs:comment` |
| `example` | `skos:example` on axiom, `skos:example` on property, auto-generated |

input.content:
# Minimal Example

This example demonstrates the simplest possible ASG configuration with a working ontology.

## File Structure

```
project/
├── config.yaml
├── ontology.owl
└── output/
    └── myapi/
        ├── openapi.yaml
        └── openapi.json
```

## Configuration File

**config.yaml:**
```yaml
name: myapi
ontologies:
  - ./ontology.owl
outputDir: ./output
rootClass: https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0
enforceRequiredness: true

templates:
  openapi:
    openapi: 3.1.0
    info:
      title: My API
      version: 1.0.0
      description: A minimal example API
```

## Ontology File

**ontology.owl:**
```xml
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<rdf:RDF xmlns="https://example.com/"
    xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
    xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
    xmlns:xml="http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"
    xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#"
    xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
    xmlns:skos="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#"
    xmlns:base="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/"
    xmlns:api="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/">

    <owl:Ontology rdf:about="https://example.com/">
        <owl:imports rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/V1.0.2"/>
    </owl:Ontology>

    <!-- APIRoot links to our API specification -->
    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/APIRoot">
        <rdfs:subClassOf>
            <owl:Restriction>
                <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/hasAPI"/>
                <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0"/>
            </owl:Restriction>
        </rdfs:subClassOf>
    </rdf:Description>

    <!-- API Specification Class -->
    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0">
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/GRLAPISpecifications"/>
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasPersonResource"/>
        <skos:prefLabel>My API V1.0</skos:prefLabel>
    </owl:Class>

    <!-- Link to Person resource -->
    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasPersonResource">
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/hasResourceSchema"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onClass rdf:resource="https://example.com/Person"/>
    </owl:Restriction>

    <!-- Tag annotation for the resource -->
    <owl:Axiom>
        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0"/>
        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasPersonResource"/>
        <api:apitag>People</api:apitag>
    </owl:Axiom>

    <!-- Person Resource Class -->
    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/Person">
        <skos:prefLabel>Person</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>A person in the system.</skos:definition>
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="personApiId"/>
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="personName"/>
        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="personEmail"/>
    </owl:Class>

    <!-- apiID restriction (required for resources) -->
    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="personApiId">
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/apiID"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
    <owl:Axiom>
        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/Person"/>
        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="personApiId"/>
        <api:apiLabel>MyAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
    </owl:Axiom>

    <!-- Name property -->
    <owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/name">
        <skos:prefLabel>Name</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>The person's full name.</skos:definition>
    </owl:DatatypeProperty>

    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="personName">
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/name"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
    <owl:Axiom>
        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/Person"/>
        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="personName"/>
        <api:apiLabel>MyAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
    </owl:Axiom>

    <!-- Email property -->
    <owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/email">
        <skos:prefLabel>Email</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>The person's email address.</skos:definition>
        <skos:example>person@example.com</skos:example>
    </owl:DatatypeProperty>

    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="personEmail">
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/email"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
    <owl:Axiom>
        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/Person"/>
        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="personEmail"/>
        <api:apiLabel>MyAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
    </owl:Axiom>
</rdf:RDF>
```

## Running ASG

```bash
java -jar asg-1.0.0-jar-with-dependencies.jar -c config.yaml
```

## Generated Output

**openapi.yaml (excerpt):**
```yaml
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: My API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: A minimal example API
  x-grl-api-label: MyAPIV1.0
  x-grl-graph-namespace: https://example.com/

tags:
  - name: Person
    description: A person in the system.

paths:
  /Person:
    summary: Person collection
    get:
      operationId: Person/collection/GET
      tags: [Person]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Person Collection
    post:
      operationId: Person/collection/POST
      tags: [Person]
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/Person'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: New Person

  /Person/{PersonId}:
    summary: Single Person
    get:
      operationId: Person/single/GET
      tags: [Person]
      parameters:
        - name: PersonId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Existing Person
    put:
      operationId: Person/single/PUT
      # ... similar structure
    patch:
      operationId: Person/single/PATCH
      # ...
    delete:
      operationId: Person/single/DELETE
      # ...

components:
  schemas:
    Person:
      type: object
      title: Person
      description: A person in the system.
      additionalProperties: false
      required:
        - apiID
        - name
        - email
      properties:
        apiID:
          type: string
          title: Api Id
          description: REST ID of the object.
          example: apiID string value
        name:
          type: string
          title: Name
          description: The person's full name.
          example: name string value
        email:
          type: array
          minItems: 1
          items:
            type: string
            title: Email
            description: The person's email address.
            example: person@example.com
```

## Key Points

1. **API Root Connection**: The `APIRoot` class must have a `hasAPI` restriction pointing to your API spec class.

2. **Resource Schema Link**: Your API spec class needs `hasResourceSchema` restrictions for each resource.

3. **apiLabel Annotations**: Every property restriction must be annotated with `apiLabel` matching your API name.

4. **apiID Property**: Every resource class must have an `apiID` data property restriction.

5. **Cardinality**: Use `qualifiedCardinality(1)` for required singular properties, `someValuesFrom` for required arrays.

input.content:
# Common Patterns

Recipe-style patterns for common ASG use cases.

## Configuration Patterns

### Multi-Environment Configuration

Use inheritance for environment-specific settings:

**base-config.yaml:**
```yaml
enforceRequiredness: true
namespaceMappings:
  - "!http://www.w3.org/.*"
  - ".*"
templates:
  openapi:
    openapi: 3.1.0
```

**dev-config.yaml:**
```yaml
extends: base-config.yaml
name: myapi_dev
outputDir: ./output/dev
templates:
  openapi:
    servers:
      - url: http://localhost:8080
        description: Development
```

**prod-config.yaml:**
```yaml
extends: base-config.yaml
name: myapi_prod
outputDir: ./output/prod
templates:
  openapi:
    servers:
      - url: https://api.example.com
        description: Production
```

### Multiple APIs from One Ontology

Define multiple API spec classes with different resources:

```yaml
# Public API
rootClass: https://example.com/PublicAPIV1.0
name: public_api
```

```yaml
# Admin API
rootClass: https://example.com/AdminAPIV1.0
name: admin_api
```

In the ontology, use different `apiLabel` values for each API.

## Ontology Patterns

### Required String Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/name"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Required, non-array string property.

### Optional String Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/nickname"/>
        <owl:maxCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:maxCardinality>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Optional, non-array property (maxItems: 1).

### Required Array Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/tags"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Required array with minItems: 1.

### Optional Array with Max Size

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/aliases"/>
        <owl:maxCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">5</owl:maxCardinality>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Optional array with maxItems: 5.

### Fixed-Size Array

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/coordinates"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">2</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#double"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Required array with minItems: 2, maxItems: 2.

### String Enum Property

```xml
<!-- Named individuals as enum values -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Active</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Inactive</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** String property with enum: [Active, Inactive].

### Reference to Another Resource

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/createdBy"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/User"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** If `User` is a resource class:
```yaml
createdBy:
  type: string
  x-grl-model-link: User
  description: Unique identifier of User
```

### Nested Object (Non-Resource)

```xml
<!-- Address class (no apiID, not a resource) -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/Address">
    <skos:prefLabel>Address</skos:prefLabel>
    <rdfs:subClassOf>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/street"/>
            <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="xsd:string"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </rdfs:subClassOf>
    <!-- more properties... -->
</owl:Class>

<!-- Reference from Person -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/address"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Address"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Nested object via $ref (not x-grl-model-link).

### DateTime Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/createdAt"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
createdAt:
  type: string
  format: date-time
  example: "2025-01-15T14:55:58+00:00"
```

### URI Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/website"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
website:
  type: string
  format: uri
  example: "http://www.example.com"
```

### Property with Default Value

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/country"/>
        <owl:hasValue rdf:datatype="xsd:string">USA</owl:hasValue>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
country:
  type: string
  default: "USA"
```

### Union Type (anyOf)

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Email"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Phone"/>
                </owl:unionOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
contact:
  anyOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Email'
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Phone'
```

## Security Patterns

### OAuth2 with Per-Resource Scopes

**config.yaml:**
```yaml
securitySchemeName: oauth2
defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}.{model}.{action}"
securityActionMappings:
  GET: read
  POST: create
  PUT: update
  PATCH: update
  DELETE: delete

templates:
  openapi:
    components:
      securitySchemes:
        oauth2:
          type: oauth2
          flows:
            authorizationCode:
              authorizationUrl: https://auth.example.com/authorize
              tokenUrl: https://auth.example.com/token
```

**Result:** Each endpoint gets security like `myapi.Employee.read`.

### Resource-Level Security Override

In ontology:
```xml
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/AdminSettings">
    <GRLAnnotations:security>admin</GRLAnnotations:security>
</owl:Class>
```

**config.yaml:**
```yaml
securityTemplate: "{api}.{security}.{action}"
```

**Result:** AdminSettings endpoints get security like `myapi.admin.read`.

### API Key Authentication

```yaml
templates:
  openapi:
    components:
      securitySchemes:
        api_key:
          type: apiKey
          in: header
          name: X-API-Key
    security:
      - api_key: []

# Disable scope-based security
defaultSecurityTemplate: null
```

## Organization Patterns

### Grouping Resources with Tags

On `hasResourceSchema` axioms:
```xml
<owl:Axiom>
    <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0"/>
    <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="rdfs:subClassOf"/>
    <owl:annotatedTarget><!-- hasResourceSchema for Employee --></owl:annotatedTarget>
    <api:apitag>Human Resources</api:apitag>
</owl:Axiom>

<owl:Axiom>
    <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0"/>
    <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="rdfs:subClassOf"/>
    <owl:annotatedTarget><!-- hasResourceSchema for Product --></owl:annotatedTarget>
    <api:apitag>Inventory</api:apitag>
</owl:Axiom>
```

**Result:** Resources grouped by tag in OpenAPI tags and operation tags.

toolUseResult.content:
# Common Patterns

Recipe-style patterns for common ASG use cases.

## Configuration Patterns

### Multi-Environment Configuration

Use inheritance for environment-specific settings:

**base-config.yaml:**
```yaml
enforceRequiredness: true
namespaceMappings:
  - "!http://www.w3.org/.*"
  - ".*"
templates:
  openapi:
    openapi: 3.1.0
```

**dev-config.yaml:**
```yaml
extends: base-config.yaml
name: myapi_dev
outputDir: ./output/dev
templates:
  openapi:
    servers:
      - url: http://localhost:8080
        description: Development
```

**prod-config.yaml:**
```yaml
extends: base-config.yaml
name: myapi_prod
outputDir: ./output/prod
templates:
  openapi:
    servers:
      - url: https://api.example.com
        description: Production
```

### Multiple APIs from One Ontology

Define multiple API spec classes with different resources:

```yaml
# Public API
rootClass: https://example.com/PublicAPIV1.0
name: public_api
```

```yaml
# Admin API
rootClass: https://example.com/AdminAPIV1.0
name: admin_api
```

In the ontology, use different `apiLabel` values for each API.

## Ontology Patterns

### Required String Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/name"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Required, non-array string property.

### Optional String Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/nickname"/>
        <owl:maxCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:maxCardinality>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Optional, non-array property (maxItems: 1).

### Required Array Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/tags"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Required array with minItems: 1.

### Optional Array with Max Size

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/aliases"/>
        <owl:maxCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">5</owl:maxCardinality>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Optional array with maxItems: 5.

### Fixed-Size Array

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/coordinates"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">2</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#double"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Required array with minItems: 2, maxItems: 2.

### String Enum Property

```xml
<!-- Named individuals as enum values -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Active</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Inactive</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** String property with enum: [Active, Inactive].

### Reference to Another Resource

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/createdBy"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/User"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** If `User` is a resource class:
```yaml
createdBy:
  type: string
  x-grl-model-link: User
  description: Unique identifier of User
```

### Nested Object (Non-Resource)

```xml
<!-- Address class (no apiID, not a resource) -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/Address">
    <skos:prefLabel>Address</skos:prefLabel>
    <rdfs:subClassOf>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/street"/>
            <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="xsd:string"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </rdfs:subClassOf>
    <!-- more properties... -->
</owl:Class>

<!-- Reference from Person -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/address"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Address"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Nested object via $ref (not x-grl-model-link).

### DateTime Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/createdAt"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
createdAt:
  type: string
  format: date-time
  example: "2025-01-15T14:55:58+00:00"
```

### URI Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/website"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
website:
  type: string
  format: uri
  example: "http://www.example.com"
```

### Property with Default Value

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/country"/>
        <owl:hasValue rdf:datatype="xsd:string">USA</owl:hasValue>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
country:
  type: string
  default: "USA"
```

### Union Type (anyOf)

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Email"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Phone"/>
                </owl:unionOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
contact:
  anyOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Email'
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Phone'
```

## Security Patterns

### OAuth2 with Per-Resource Scopes

**config.yaml:**
```yaml
securitySchemeName: oauth2
defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}.{model}.{action}"
securityActionMappings:
  GET: read
  POST: create
  PUT: update
  PATCH: update
  DELETE: delete

templates:
  openapi:
    components:
      securitySchemes:
        oauth2:
          type: oauth2
          flows:
            authorizationCode:
              authorizationUrl: https://auth.example.com/authorize
              tokenUrl: https://auth.example.com/token
```

**Result:** Each endpoint gets security like `myapi.Employee.read`.

### Resource-Level Security Override

In ontology:
```xml
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/AdminSettings">
    <GRLAnnotations:security>admin</GRLAnnotations:security>
</owl:Class>
```

**config.yaml:**
```yaml
securityTemplate: "{api}.{security}.{action}"
```

**Result:** AdminSettings endpoints get security like `myapi.admin.read`.

### API Key Authentication

```yaml
templates:
  openapi:
    components:
      securitySchemes:
        api_key:
          type: apiKey
          in: header
          name: X-API-Key
    security:
      - api_key: []

# Disable scope-based security
defaultSecurityTemplate: null
```

## Organization Patterns

### Grouping Resources with Tags

On `hasResourceSchema` axioms:
```xml
<owl:Axiom>
    <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0"/>
    <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="rdfs:subClassOf"/>
    <owl:annotatedTarget><!-- hasResourceSchema for Employee --></owl:annotatedTarget>
    <api:apitag>Human Resources</api:apitag>
</owl:Axiom>

<owl:Axiom>
    <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0"/>
    <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="rdfs:subClassOf"/>
    <owl:annotatedTarget><!-- hasResourceSchema for Product --></owl:annotatedTarget>
    <api:apitag>Inventory</api:apitag>
</owl:Axiom>
```

**Result:** Resources grouped by tag in OpenAPI tags and operation tags.

input.content:
# Troubleshooting

Common issues and solutions when using ASG.

## Configuration Issues

### "Name must be alphanumeric with underscores only"

**Problem:** The `name` field contains invalid characters.

**Solution:** Use only letters, numbers, and underscores:
```yaml
# Bad
name: my-api

# Good
name: my_api
```

### "Only one namespace mapping can be specified"

**Problem:** Multiple namespace mapping targets defined.

**Solution:** Use a single target namespace with multiple patterns:
```yaml
# Bad
namespaceMappings:
  https://example.com/api1/:
    - https://example.com/.*
  https://example.com/api2/:
    - https://other.com/.*

# Good
namespaceMappings:
  https://example.com/api/:
    - https://example.com/.*
    - https://other.com/.*
```

Or use a list (target inferred):
```yaml
namespaceMappings:
  - https://example.com/.*
  - https://other.com/.*
```

### "Cannot find rootClass"

**Problem:** The specified `rootClass` doesn't exist or isn't a valid API spec class.

**Solution:**
1. Check the IRI is correct (case-sensitive)
2. Verify the class is a subclass of `GRLAPISpecifications`
3. Ensure the ontology imports are loaded correctly

### Relative paths not resolving

**Problem:** Ontology files or catalog not found.

**Solution:** Paths are relative to the config file location:
```yaml
# If config is at /project/config/api.yaml
# and ontology is at /project/ontologies/my.owl

ontologies:
  - ../ontologies/my.owl  # Correct
  - /ontologies/my.owl    # Wrong - looks for /ontologies/
```

Use `${configDir}` for clarity:
```yaml
ontologies:
  - ${configDir}/../ontologies/my.owl
```

## Ontology Issues

### "No properties with apiLabel annotation found"

**Problem:** Properties aren't being included in the generated schema.

**Causes:**
1. Missing `apiLabel` annotations on SubClassOf axioms
2. Mismatched API name in annotations
3. Annotations on wrong element

**Solution:** Verify axiom annotations:
```xml
<!-- The apiLabel goes on the AXIOM, not the class or property -->
<owl:Axiom>
    <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/Employee"/>
    <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
    <owl:annotatedTarget><!-- blank node ID of the restriction --></owl:annotatedTarget>
    <www:apiLabel>MyAPIV1.0</www:apiLabel>  <!-- Must match rootClass name -->
</owl:Axiom>
```

### Resource not appearing as endpoint

**Problem:** A class isn't generating API endpoints.

**Causes:**
1. Missing `hasResourceSchema` restriction from API spec class
2. Class doesn't have `apiID` property restriction
3. Missing `apiLabel` annotation on `apiID` restriction

**Solution:** Ensure the class:
1. Is linked from API spec via `hasResourceSchema`
2. Has an `apiID` data property restriction
3. Has `apiLabel` annotation on the `apiID` restriction

### Properties showing as arrays unexpectedly

**Problem:** Properties are arrays when they should be singular.

**Cause:** `someValuesFrom` always implies an array.

**Solution:** Use `qualifiedCardinality(1)` for singular required properties:
```xml
<!-- Array (someValuesFrom) -->
<owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="xsd:string"/>

<!-- Singular (exactCardinality) -->
<owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
<owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
```

### Missing required properties

**Problem:** Properties with cardinality >= 1 aren't marked required.

**Solution:** Enable `enforceRequiredness`:
```yaml
enforceRequiredness: true
```

### Object property shows as string instead of $ref

**Problem:** An object property shows a string type with `x-grl-model-link` instead of a schema reference.

**Cause:** The range class IS a resource (has `apiID`), so it's treated as a reference.

**Explanation:** This is correct behavior. When a class is a resource (has its own API endpoints), references to it become identifiers (strings) with `x-grl-model-link`, not nested objects.

**If you want nested objects:** Remove the `apiID` restriction from the range class so it's not treated as a resource.

## Generation Issues

### Import not resolved

**Problem:** Ontology import fails to load.

**Solutions:**
1. Use a catalog file:
   ```yaml
   catalogFile: ./catalog-v001.xml
   ```
2. Ensure imported ontology files are accessible
3. Check network access for remote imports

### Namespace flattening issues

**Problem:** IRIs aren't being flattened correctly.

**Debugging:**
```bash
java -jar asg.jar -c config.yaml -G
```

Check `ontology-flat.owl` in the output directory to see the flattened result.

**Solution:** Adjust namespace mappings. Use `!` prefix to exclude:
```yaml
namespaceMappings:
  - "!http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#.*"      # Exclude OWL
  - "!http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#.*"   # Exclude XSD
  - ".*"                                       # Include everything else
```

### OutOfMemoryError

**Problem:** JVM runs out of memory with large ontologies.

**Solution:** Increase heap size:
```bash
java -Xmx4g -jar asg.jar -c config.yaml
```

## Output Issues

### Missing descriptions or titles

**Problem:** Generated schema lacks descriptions or titles.

**Solution:** Add SKOS annotations to ontology:
```xml
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/Employee">
    <skos:prefLabel>Employee</skos:prefLabel>       <!-- becomes title -->
    <skos:definition>A company employee.</skos:definition>  <!-- becomes description -->
</owl:Class>
```

### Generic examples

**Problem:** Examples are auto-generated and not meaningful.

**Solution:** Add `skos:example` annotations:
```xml
<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/email">
    <skos:example>john.doe@example.com</skos:example>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>
```

Or on the axiom for context-specific examples.

### Security requirements not generated

**Problem:** Endpoints don't have security requirements.

**Solution:**
1. Define security scheme in template:
   ```yaml
   templates:
     openapi:
       components:
         securitySchemes:
           oauth2:
             type: oauth2
             flows: ...
   ```
2. Configure security templates:
   ```yaml
   defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}.{model}.{action}"
   ```
3. Or add `security` annotation to resource classes.

## Debugging Tips

1. **Check effective-config.yaml** - Review the merged configuration in the output directory.

2. **Use -G flag** - Stop after flattening to verify namespace mappings:
   ```bash
   java -jar asg.jar -c config.yaml -G
   ```

3. **Examine ontology files** - Review `ontology-merged.owl` to see what ASG is working with.

4. **Enable logging** - Check console output for warnings about missing annotations.

5. **Validate ontology** - Use Protégé or another OWL tool to check ontology validity.

```

### Missing required properties

**Problem:** Properties with cardinality >= 1 aren't marked required.

**Solution:** Enable `enforceRequiredness`:
```yaml
enforceRequiredness: true
```

### Object property shows as string instead of $ref

**Problem:** An object property shows a string type with `x-grl-model-link` instead of a schema reference.

**Cause:** The range class IS a resource (has `apiID`), so it's treated as a reference.

**Explanation:** This is correct behavior. When a class is a resource (has its own API endpoints), references to it become identifiers (strings) with `x-grl-model-link`, not nested objects.

**If you want nested objects:** Remove the `apiID` restriction from the range class so it's not treated as a resource.

## Generation Issues

### Import not resolved

**Problem:** Ontology import fails to load.

**Solutions:**
1. Use a catalog file:
   ```yaml
   catalogFile: ./catalog-v001.xml
   ```
2. Ensure imported ontology files are accessible
3. Check network access for remote imports

### Namespace flattening issues

**Problem:** IRIs aren't being flattened correctly.

**Debugging:**
```bash
java -jar asg.jar -c config.yaml -G
```

Check `ontology-flat.owl` in the output directory to see the flattened result.

**Solution:** Adjust namespace mappings. Use `!` prefix to exclude:
```yaml
namespaceMappings:
  - "!http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#.*"      # Exclude OWL
  - "!http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#.*"   # Exclude XSD
  - ".*"                                       # Include everything else
```

### OutOfMemoryError

**Problem:** JVM runs out of memory with large ontologies.

**Solution:** Increase heap size:
```bash
java -Xmx4g -jar asg.jar -c config.yaml
```

## Output Issues

### Missing descriptions or titles

**Problem:** Generated schema lacks descriptions or titles.

**Solution:** Add SKOS annotations to ontology:
```xml
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/Employee">
    <skos:prefLabel>Employee</skos:prefLabel>       <!-- becomes title -->
    <skos:definition>A company employee.</skos:definition>  <!-- becomes description -->
</owl:Class>
```

### Generic examples

**Problem:** Examples are auto-generated and not meaningful.

**Solution:** Add `skos:example` annotations:
```xml
<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/email">
    <skos:example>john.doe@example.com</skos:example>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>
```

Or on the axiom for context-specific examples.

### Security requirements not generated

**Problem:** Endpoints don't have security requirements.

**Solution:**
1. Define security scheme in template:
   ```yaml
   templates:
     openapi:
       components:
         securitySchemes:
           oauth2:
             type: oauth2
             flows: ...
   ```
2. Configure security templates:
   ```yaml
   defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}.{model}.{action}"
   ```
3. Or add `security` annotation to resource classes.

## Debugging Tips

1. **Check effective-config.yaml** - Review the merged configuration in the output directory.

2. **Use -G flag** - Stop after flattening to verify namespace mappings:
   ```bash
   java -jar asg.jar -c config.yaml -G
   ```

3. **Examine ontology files** - Review `ontology-merged.owl` to see what ASG is working with.

4. **Enable logging** - Check console output for warnings about missing annotations.

5. **Validate ontology** - Use Protégé or another OWL tool to check ontology validity.

    <owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/cardinality/exactCardOne">
        <skos:prefLabel>Exact Card One</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Property with exactCardinality 1</skos:definition>
    </owl:DatatypeProperty>

    <owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/cardinality/exactCardTwo">
        <skos:prefLabel>Exact Card Two</skos:prefLabel>
        <skos:definition>Property with exactCardinality 2</skos:definition>

    <!-- CardinalityTest Resource Class -->

    <!-- maxCardinality 3 restriction -->

        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>

        <owl:onClass rdf:resource="https://example.com/datatypes/DatatypesTest"/>

    <owl:Axiom>

    </owl:Axiom>

    <!-- Data Properties -->
    <owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/datatypes/stringProp">
        <skos:prefLabel>String Prop</skos:prefLabel>
    </owl:DatatypeProperty>

    <owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/datatypes/integerProp">
        <skos:prefLabel>Integer Prop</skos:prefLabel>
    </owl:DatatypeProperty>

    </owl:DatatypeProperty>
